task_completion.description: Set the custom threshold range to 150 HU (Min) and 700 HU (Max).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The thresholding or segmentation panel in InVesalius shows the minimum value set to 150 and the maximum value set to 700.

task_completion.description: Create a segmentation mask using the specified custom threshold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new mask appears in the 'Masks' list, and/or the 2D slice views show highlighted regions corresponding to the thresholded alveolar structures.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface from the segmentation mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer displays a generated 3D model of the bone/alveolar structures, and a new surface item is visible in the 'Surfaces' list.
A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'alveolar_study.inv3' in the Documents folder, or the main InVesalius window title bar updated to reflect the 'alveolar_study' project name.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as a binary STL file to /home/ga/Documents/alveolar_surface.stl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D Surface' dialog showing the filename 'alveolar_surface.stl' in the Documents folder with the STL format selected, or a success message indicating the export.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the InVesalius GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through InVesalius menus, panels, and dialogs rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the 3D surface and 2D slices display genuine cranial/alveolar structures.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 2D slices and 3D viewer should show complex, organic human bone structures (cranial base/jaw), not generic shapes, synthetic patterns, or empty screens.

integrity.description: Verify that saving and exporting were done through the application's native file dialogs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard file browser dialogs triggered from within InVesalius should be visible for the save and export steps, rather than terminal 'touch' or 'mv' commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The software used is InVesalius 3.0. The dataset is a genuine cranial CT scan containing 110 DICOM slices. The task strictly requires setting a CUSTOM threshold range of 150 to 700 HU. This specific range corresponds to alveolar/dental bone density (D4-D3 range) and is distinctly different from standard InVesalius presets (e.g., standard Bone is 226-3071 HU, Spongy is 148-661 HU). The VLM must verify that the agent manually inputted or adjusted the sliders to exactly 150 and 700, rather than selecting a preset.
